name: AutoWIFI
summary: Wireless penetration testing framework. Automates the full attack chain - recon through exploitation - with a clean terminal interface.

documentation.readme: Wireless penetration testing framework. Automates the full attack chain - recon through exploitation - with a clean terminal interface.
usage_sections: Features: - Multi-vector attacks - WEP (ARP replay, fragmentation, chopchop), WPA/WPA2 (handshake capture, PMKID), WPS (pixie dust, PIN brute force) - Smart target selection - Auto-recommends attack vectors based on target encryption and configuration - Live scanning - Real-time network di

arguments.name: --backend
arguments.description: CLI flag '--backend' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: -S
arguments.description: CLI flag '-S' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Reconnaissance system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for AutoWIFI (momenbasel/AutoWIFI). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for AutoWIFI. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

examples: sudo apt install -y aircrack-ng reaver bully hcxdumptool hcxtools hashcat macchanger mdk4 tshark
sudo pacman -S aircrack-ng reaver hashcat hcxdumptool hcxtools macchanger wireshark-cli
Launches the full TUI with menu-driven workflow - scan, select target, attack, crack.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with xransum/phantex (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with smoorthi_uno/Shodan_Python_Project (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with sergioamsilva/wifi-security-toolkit (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web, cloud, iots] in the Reconnaissance category. Fits when the engagement needs: Wireless penetration testing framework. Automates the full attack chain - recon through exploitation - with a clean terminal interface.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Wireless penetration testing framework. Automates the full attack chain - recon through exploitation - with a clean terminal interface.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip install autowifi; git clone https://github.com/momenbasel/AutoWIFI.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sudo apt install -y aircrack-ng reaver bully hcxdumptool hcxtools hashcat macchanger mdk4 tshark`. Further invocations from upstream: `sudo pacman -S aircrack-ng reaver hashcat hcxdumptool hcxtools macchanger wireshark-cli`; `sudo autowifi --version`. Wireless penetration testing framework. Automates the full attack chain - recon through exploitation - with a clean terminal interface.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.
